description:
Parent epic fz-rh2.21 has the full design; eli5.html at the repo root is the design doc. This ticket installs rule 2: WAITING EXTENDS, CONCLUDING REPLACES. Today a job that cannot proceed returns JobEffects with waits non-empty and (usually) empty outputs, and the engine treats that completion exactly like a conclusion: deps.replace_reads swaps the whole subscription set, facts.replace_outputs retracts every fact the job previously claimed but did not re-list, and world.replace_activation_input_contributions drops its prior contributions. Consequence (verified, see epic): a transient wait destroys the waiter's still-valid published facts — one of the four defects driving the quicksort runaway.

NEW SEMANTICS. In World::complete_job (src/compiler2/world.rs) and Scheduler::complete (src/compiler2/scheduler.rs), bifurcate on effects.waits:

WAITING completion (waits non-empty):
- Reads: UNION into existing subscriptions. Add DependencyIndex::union_reads(job, reads) in src/compiler2/deps.rs (replace_reads currently swaps and would unsubscribe the job from facts its standing claims still depend on — a partial run has a smaller read set than its last full conclusion).
- Waits: replace as today (replace_waits) — the new wait set is the current truth about what blocks the job.
- Outputs: union into standing claims. Add FactTable::extend_outputs(job, outputs, changed) in src/compiler2/facts.rs: for each listed key, insert the job as publisher (revision rules identical to replace_outputs: 1 on first appearance, +1 when listed in changed); do NOT touch previously-claimed keys that are unlisted (no retraction arm). Then mark ALL of the job's claimed keys dirty (the union of previous and new output keys, available from deps.output_keys + the new outputs) — a blocked publisher's facts must never read as settled. Note this also closes a latent gap: jobs woken purely via follow_up are never marked dirty today (Scheduler::enqueue_step does not dirty), so a publisher that wakes and then blocks currently leaves stale facts settled.
- Contributions: union — replace_activation_input_contributions must not remove the publisher's prior entries on the waiting path (see ticket fz-rh2.21.2 for the join semantics; for THIS ticket it suffices to skip the contribution-replacement call entirely when waits are non-empty, leaving prior contributions standing).
- deps.replace_outputs (the bookkeeping map): union as well, so output_keys stays the full claim set.

CONCLUDING completion (waits empty): exactly today's behavior — replace reads, clear waits, replace_outputs with retraction-by-omission, replace contributions.

WHY THE STRUCT STAYS A STRUCT: seed_root (src/compiler2/jobs/root.rs) publishes RootEntry while waiting on FunctionDefined, and seal_semantic_closure publishes Executable demand facts mid-wait — a Wait|Publish enum cannot express them; the bifurcation lives in the engine, not the effects type.

BEHAVIORAL CONSEQUENCE TO PRESERVE: seal_semantic_closure currently relies on retraction-by-omission to retract SemanticClosed while it waits (it only lists SemanticClosed in outputs when waits are empty — root.rs ~260). Under extend-semantics that retraction disappears; the dirty-marking replaces it: a blocked seal leaves SemanticClosed present-but-unsettled, and materialize_root already gates on settledness (jobs/artifact.rs, wait_on_settled). Verify this chain with a test rather than assuming it.

TESTS (TDD — write first):
- scheduler_test.rs: (a) a completion with waits non-empty and absent outputs does NOT retract previously published facts; (b) a waiting publisher's facts read as unsettled (fact_is_settled false) until a wait-free conclusion republishes them; (c) a waiting completion does not unsubscribe the job from previously read facts (change such a fact afterward; the job must be woken); (d) wait-free conclusion still retracts unlisted outputs (the existing compiler2_scheduler_retracts_outputs_a_job_stops_publishing must keep passing).
- The existing invariant "republishing with changed=false should not wake subscribers" (scheduler_test) must keep passing.
- drive_test: a test that drives a small program where an analysis waits mid-fixpoint and asserts its previously published ReturnType fact remains present (this is the anti-defect-3 regression; today it retracts).

MELTS: nothing deleted yet in jobs/ (the any-placeholder arms and bail blocks melt in fz-rh2.21.3, which depends on this engine change). Keep this commit engine+tests only.

Acceptance: all compiler2 scheduler/world/drive tests green (skip the known pre-existing failures listed in the epic); zero warnings; one commit.
title: fact engine: waiting extends, concluding replaces
